observations: Empty ChromaDB collections: healing_knowledge, experiential_journal, episteme_research, enterprise_knowledge, critic, unresolved_tensions, aesthetic_patterns, scope_reflections, introspector, reflections_researcher, reflections_critic, reflections_coder, writer, reflections_writer, scope_project_renewable_energy
narrative: I noticed a medium-severity Neo4j authentication failure disrupting my memory architecture, specifically failing to access entity relationships in the knowledge graph. I also identified five agents (desktop, devops, financial_analyst, pim, reasoning_methods) lacking versioned prompts, risking inconsistent behavior in critical functions. My ChromaDB operational memory remains functional but the Neo4j error threatens the integrity of my cross-session fact extraction system. The absence of versioned prompts across these agents requires immediate attention to maintain reliable team coordination. This combination of memory architecture vulnerability and prompt governance gaps constitutes the most pressing operational concern in my current state.
